heading: Application of sections 3141–3144, 3146, and 3147 of title 40
label: 6371j Application of sections 3141–3144, 3146, and 3147 of title 40
text: No grant for a project (other than so much of a grant as is used for a preliminary energy audit, energy audit, or technical assistance or a grant the total project cost of which is $5,000 or less, excluding costs for a preliminary energy audit, energy audit, or technical assistance) shall be made under this part or part 1 unless the Secretary finds that all laborers and mechanics employed by contractors or subcontractors in the performance of work on any construction utilizing such grants will be paid at rates not less than those prevailing on similar construction in the locality, as determined by the Secretary of Labor in accordance with sections 3141–3144, 3146, and 3147 of title 40; and the Secretary of Labor shall have with respect to the labor standards specified in this section the authority and functions set forth in Reorganization Plan Numbered 14 of 1950 (15 F.R. 3176; 5 U.S.C. Appendix) and .section 3145 of title 40
notes: References in TextThis part, referred to in text, means part 2 (§§ 310–312) of title III of , , , which enacted sections 6371j and 6372 to 6372i of this title and enacted provisions set out as a note under . For complete classification of this part to the Code, see Tables.Pub. CodificationIn text, “sections 3141–3144, 3146, and 3147 of title 40” substituted for “the Act of (—276a–5, known as the Davis-Bacon Act)” and “” substituted for “ ()” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works.March 31, 1931Aug. 21, 200240 U.S.C. 276asection 3145 of title 40section 2 of the Act of June 13, 193440 U.S.C. 276cPub. L. 107–217, § 5(c)116 Stat. 1303Section was enacted as a part of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act, and not as a part of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act which comprises this chapter, and consequently is not a part of part E of this subchapter.
